top_signals.title: Odd Lots: Grace Shao on How Chinese AI is Different (Podcast)
top_signals.summary: China’s AI industry has changed a lot since DeepSeek released its cheap frontier model last year, and briefly sent US tech stocks falling. After being locked out of the most advanced chips, Chinese companies are now allowed to buy some Nvidia H200s. In fact, many of the big Chinese tech companies — like Baidu — are making a push to become full-stack players, with their own chips, models, and cloud infrastructure. Today’s guest is Grace Shao, an independent AI researcher and the author of the AI
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Police raid malware network tied to Russia's Evil Corp hacker group
top_signals.summary: An international operation targeted the SocGholish botnet, which has been linked to the Russia-based cybercrime group Evil Corp.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Grace Shao on What the World Should Know About Chinese AI
top_signals.summary: This is how the Chinese ecosystem is different.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Japan to fully digitalize airport customs system by 2030
top_signals.summary: The phasing-out of paper customs declaration forms is among measures eyed by the Finance Ministry to handle the inbound movement of people and goods more efficiently.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
